diagrams.title: Three-Schema Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the three levels of database abstraction: External, Conceptual, and Internal schemas, with arrows indicating data independence between levels.
diagrams.purpose: Understanding data abstraction and the three-level architecture of DBMS

diagrams.title: Schema vs Instance Comparison
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the difference between database schema (structure) and instance (actual data) using a Student table example.
elements: Table Header (Schema) - Columns: RollNo, Name, Age
Box showing Schema definition and Instance data
diagrams.purpose: Clarifying the difference between static schema and dynamic instance

diagrams.title: Data Model Types
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of four main data models: Hierarchical (tree), Network (graph), Relational (table), Object-Oriented (class diagram).
elements: Network: Connected graph with multiple links
Relational: Table with rows and columns
Object-Oriented: Class with attributes and methods
diagrams.purpose: Comparing structure and representation of different data models

diagrams.title: Data Independence Flow
diagrams.description: Shows how changes at lower levels affect higher levels and the protection offered by data independence.
elements: Internal Schema (Storage) - changes don't affect Conceptual
Conceptual Schema (Logical) - changes don't affect External
External Schema (User View) - protected from lower changes
Arrows showing impact direction and protection barriers
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how data independence works in practice
